text: Open Source Intel U.S. Vice President JD Vance will be delivering remarks in Islamabad, Pakistan shortly. Contributed by @AZ_Intel_ . https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2043132180323262946/photo/1 tweet
Faytuks News JD Vance to speak after first day of U.S.-Iran talks in Islamabad https://www.youtube.com/live/4fwqBgAhZPo tweet
OSINTdefender The presser with U.S. Vice President JD Vance is set to begin shortly. https://twitter.com/sentdefender/status/2043130824195735939/photo/1 tweet

OSINTdefender A presser with U.S. Vice President JD Vance is reportedly set to begin in the coming minutes. Vice President Vance has been in negotiations for the past 16 hours, as a part of in person trilateral talks between the U.S., Pakistan, and Iran over this past month’s conflict in the Middle East. tweet
OSINTtechnical Vance is expected to hold a press conference after a marathon negotiation session with the Iranians in Pakistan. It is 5:50 AM. tweet
⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 CBS: J.D. Vance is expected to speak at a press conference in Islamabad shortly. @wfwitness
🇬🇧 ⚡️ UK Police arrest more than 500 people during a demonstration against the ban on Palestine Action in central London. The age of those arrested ranged from 18 to 87.

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇵🇰🇮🇷Tasnim: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran in Islamabad have been extended by one day. Negotiations will resume later in the morning after both sides accepted a Pakistani proposal. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 🇵🇰 Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Baqaei said negotiations between Iran and the U.S. in Islamabad have been intense and ongoing, with indirect exchanges continuing throughout the day via Pakistani mediation. He stated that discussions over the past 24 hours have covered key issues including the Strait of Hormuz, Iran’s nuclear programme, war reparations, sanctions relief, and a complete end to the conflict. Baqaei stressed that Iran remains committed to defending its national interests, while warning that the success of the diplomatic process depends on the seriousness and good faith of the U.S., as well as respect for Iran’s rights. @wfwitness

Nuno Felix RT @michaeldweiss : Good article but this bit isn’t exactly true. “Turkey has historically been very wary of its large neighbor and is an important arms seller to Ukraine. However, while it sells weapons, it gives almost no military and humanitarian aid to Kyiv.” Much of what Turkey has given Ukraine it has done so quietly or covertly. Ankara was to provide Kyiv with cluster bombs. It also gave MLRS in 2022, and TRLG-230 laser-guided surface-to-surface missiles. Also, I seem to recall a famous Ukrainian ditty about a certain drone system which played a crucial role in the Battle of Kyiv… https://www.politico.eu/article/ukraine-volodymyr-zelenskyy-warms-up-with-black-sea-strongman-turkiye/ tweet

Open Source Intel Regarding negotiations with Iran, a senior White House official told the vice president’s press pool: “15 hours and counting!” Faytuks Network reports. tweet

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇵🇰 Al-Jazeera: Negotiations between the United States and Iran were conducted directly, with participation from Pakistan, with the Pakistani delegation put forward a proposal aimed at regulating navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. A Pakistani source reports the proposal includes the implementation of joint patrols in the strategic waterway as part of efforts to ensure safe passage and stability. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇺🇸 CNN: The status of the Strait of Hormuz will remain unchanged until Iran and the United States agree on a 'common framework' for negotiations. An Iranian security source claims that, what he called excessive deamnds, from the U.S. over the critical shipping route have blocked progress toward a shared basis for talks, warning that the Strait will remain closed until the U.S. adopts a more realistic approach. They report that Tehran has made clear that without an agreed framework, even previously discussed arrangements on limited ship passage will not be implemented. They added that Iran shown their ability to reinforce restrictions. @wfwitness
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Fars correspondent dispatched to Islamabad: Negotiations will continue from Sunday morning.

Negotiations have been ongoing for 14 hours so far. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇵🇰 🇮🇷 Tasnim: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran in Islamabad have been extended by one day. 🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: 🖌 Christ is Risen! Happy Holy Pascha! Pascha is our main symbol of hope. Light is stronger than darkness. Life is stronger than death. We believe in this — and it is precisely this that gives us strength. Today this meaning is felt with particular acuteness, especially by those who stand shoulder to shoulder with death. Somewhere on the front lines our comrades are celebrating the Paschal night far from home — and it is precisely there, in this proximity to the very edge, that prayer gains true power, hope becomes not consolation, but support, and thoughts of loved ones warm in a way no fire ever could. Reporter: Iran is not listening to your threats about reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Trump: How do you know that? Reporter: Because it’s still blocked. Trump: Why do you say that? You don’t know anything. Who are you with? Reporter: NBC News. Trump: NBC? That’s fake news.
Trump on China sending weapons to Iran: If China does that, China is going to have big problems.
Trump: We are in very deep negotiations with Iran. We win regardless.
Trump: We win, regardless what happens, we win. Let’s see what happens. Maybe they will make a deal, maybe they won’t. It doesn’t matter. From the standpoint of America, we win.

⚡️ 🇰🇼 The Kuwaiti police announced they foiled a plot involving the financing of terrorist organisations and threats to national security, leading to the arrest of 24 people. They accuse them of using commercial and professional fronts to launder funds, and relied on coordinated methods by air and land, distributing funds among multiple individuals to avoid detection. One of those detained had already had their citizenship revoked, while eight additional fugitives abroad were identified, including one stripped of citizenship. @wfwitness

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 Some serious disagreements between the Iranian and American delegations in the negotiations still remain. The ball is in the American court to replace ambition with a realistic approach by overcoming the usual excesses. — Tasnim
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Another round of in-person talks between Iran and the US in Islamabad, Pakistan has ended — Tasnim. The two sides are once again exchanging texts with the presence of expert teams.

↩️ Reply to DD Geopolitics: "🇺🇸 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 “If China does that, China’s going to have big problems.” Trump speaking to reporters outside the White House, responding to reports that China is preparing to send man-portable air-defense systems (MANPADS) to Iran. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics | Socials…" 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Trump on the Islamabad talks: "Whether we make a deal or not makes no difference to me." He claims the US is currently sweeping the strait for mines laid by "water dropper" while his Vice President is currently in Pakistan negotiating. "We're opening up the strait even though we don't use it, for other countries that are afraid, or weak, or cheap." NATO, he says, was no help. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Iran's Embassy in Austria to Hegseth: “ It may not win him the next war, but at least he'll show up in the right place.”

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Iranian source to CNN: — Iran says it is not in a hurry and warns that until a framework is agreed, the current restrictions remain - agreed ship numbers will not be allowed through . — Source adds: If U.S. negotiators fail to act realistically, the Strait stays closed. Iran has demonstrated its capability, and continued miscalculation will harm the U.S.

🇺🇸 🇨🇳 🇮🇷 “If China does that, China’s going to have big problems.” Trump speaking to reporters outside the White House, responding to reports that China is preparing to send man-portable air-defense systems (MANPADS) to Iran. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 No major shift in negotiations so far — Tasnim reporter in Islamabad — Source: Talks stalled for hours due to U.S. excessive demands, while some Western media are overstating optimism to influence energy prices. — Despite early expert-level progress, no significant change yet, the Strait of Hormuz and other issues remain key points of dispute.

"The prevailing feeling currently is that Iran is winning economically and controlling the Strait of Hormuz, collecting fees from ships." Former Pentagon analyst Michael Maloof: Trump was very surprised by the level of Iranian resistance. Iran is waging asymmetric war, it cannot confront plane for plane or missile for missile, so it resorted to other methods the United States could not adapt to. Trump says "this situation must not continue," but the situation continues and he has taken no vital steps. He's looking for an exit but doesn't want to create conditions that will cause negotiators to walk out. Meanwhile, Vance is dealing with experienced Iranian negotiators like Qalibaf, who adopts hardline positions protecting his country's interests. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 “We defeated Iran but we will negotiate with them!”

↩️ Reply to ResistanceTrench mirror: "🇮🇷⚡️🇮🇱🇺🇸 Senior Iran’s Navy officer: — The destroyer that tried to enter the Strait of Hormuz today is an "Arleigh Burke" class, which is the most advanced American warship. — At the same time as the destroyer's movement, Trump sent a message that the strait…" 🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 Bloomberg, quoting a regional intelligence official: Two US Navy Arleigh Burke-class destroyers attempted to pass through the Strait of Hormuz but were forced to turn back after being threatened by IRGC and a drone was launched towards the ships.
